  "text": "The gRPC health check protocol compliance in Envoy (v1.31.2) is implemented primarily in two C++ source files within the extensions directory:\n\n1. **source/extensions/health_checkers/grpc/health_checker_impl.h** — Declares the key classes:\n   - `GrpcHealthCheckerFactory`: factory registered as 'envoy.health_checkers.grpc' that creates gRPC health checker instances.\n   - `GrpcHealthCheckerImpl`: the core implementation class, extending `HealthCheckerImplBase`. Holds a reference to the `grpc.health.v1.Health.Check` Protobuf method descriptor, optional `service_name_` and `authority_value_`, and a request headers parser for initial metadata.\n   - `GrpcActiveHealthCheckSession`: nested struct handling per-host health check sessions. Implements `Http::ResponseDecoder` and `Http::StreamCallbacks` to process gRPC responses.\n   - `ProdGrpcHealthCheckerImpl`: production subclass that allocates a real HTTP/2 codec client.\n\n2. **source/extensions/health_checkers/grpc/health_checker_impl.cc** — Implements the protocol logic:\n   - `GrpcHealthCheckerImpl::GrpcHealthCheckerImpl`: constructor looks up `grpc.health.v1.Health.Check` method from the Protobuf descriptor pool, reads `service_name` and `authority` from config.\n   - `GrpcActiveHealthCheckSession::onInterval()`: sends a `grpc.health.v1.HealthCheckRequest` (with optional `service` field set from `service_name_`) via HTTP/2, using `Grpc::Common::prepareHeaders` to set the path `/grpc.health.v1.Health/Check`.\n   - `GrpcActiveHealthCheckSession::decodeHeaders()`: validates HTTP/200 response and gRPC content-type; handles early gRPC status in trailers-only responses (e.g., `unimplemented` from grpc-go).\n   - `GrpcActiveHealthCheckSession::decodeData()`: decodes gRPC frames and parses `grpc.health.v1.HealthCheckResponse` protobuf messages.\n   - `GrpcActiveHealthCheckSession::decodeTrailers()`: extracts gRPC status from HTTP/2 trailers and calls `onRpcComplete`.\n   - `GrpcActiveHealthCheckSession::isHealthCheckSucceeded()`: maps response to health status — returns `true` only if gRPC status is `OK` **and** `HealthCheckResponse.status == SERVING`.\n   - `GrpcActiveHealthCheckSession::logHealthCheckStatus()`: logs SERVING / NOT_SERVING / UNKNOWN / SERVICE_UNKNOWN response status values.\n   - `GrpcActiveHealthCheckSession::onRpcComplete()`: calls `handleSuccess` or `handleFailure` based on `isHealthCheckSucceeded`.\n\nThe `grpc.health.v1` proto definitions (defining `HealthCheckRequest`, `HealthCheckResponse`, `ServingStatus` enum, and `Health` service with `Check` and `Watch` RPCs) are found in the gRPC repo at:\n- **src/proto/grpc/health/v1/health.proto** (in sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e)\n- **src/python/grpcio_health_checking/grpc_health/v1/health.proto** (in sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e)\n\nEnvoy's implementation uses the compiled C++ protobuf types (`grpc::health::v1::HealthCheckRequest`, `grpc::health::v1::HealthCheckResponse`, `grpc::health::v1::HealthCheckResponse::SERVING`, etc.) from this proto via the `grpc_health_proto` external dependency."
}

## gRPC Health Check Protocol Compliance in Envoy

### Core Implementation Files (sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `source/extensions/health_checkers/grpc/health_checker_impl.h` | Class declarations |
| `source/extensions/health_checkers/grpc/health_checker_impl.cc` | Protocol implementation |
| `source/extensions/health_checkers/grpc/BUILD` | Bazel build definition |

### Key Classes & Symbols

- **`GrpcHealthCheckerFactory`** — registered factory (`envoy.health_checkers.grpc`)
- **`GrpcHealthCheckerImpl`** — core implementation; constructor binds to `grpc.health.v1.Health.Check` via Protobuf descriptor pool
- **`GrpcActiveHealthCheckSession`** — per-host session handling the full gRPC request/response lifecycle
- **`ProdGrpcHealthCheckerImpl`** — production subclass allocating real HTTP/2 codec

### Protocol Flow

1. `onInterval()` — sends `grpc.health.v1.HealthCheckRequest` (with optional `service` name) to path `/grpc.health.v1.Health/Check`
2. `decodeHeaders()` / `decodeData()` / `decodeTrailers()` — parse the HTTP/2+gRPC response
3. `isHealthCheckSucceeded()` — host is healthy only if gRPC status is `OK` **and** `HealthCheckResponse.status == SERVING`
4. `logHealthCheckStatus()` — maps `SERVING`, `NOT_SERVING`, `UNKNOWN`, `SERVICE_UNKNOWN` to log strings

### Proto Definition (sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e)

- `src/proto/grpc/health/v1/health.proto` — canonical definition of `HealthCheckRequest`, `HealthCheckResponse`, `ServingStatus` enum, and the `Health` service
